info.title: MaintenancePizza API
info.description:
Ticket system microservice for store maintenance. An authenticated user files a ticket describing the issues in a store; dispatchers then drive each issue through its lifecycle (diagnosis, technician assignment, attendance, parts, pay/driving, warranty) until completion.

Key design notes:
- All endpoints require a Sanctum bearer token (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`). There are no login/registration endpoints in this service — tokens are issued elsewhere.
- Most endpoints are store-scoped under `/api/stores/{store}`, where `{store}` is the store NUMBER (e.g. `03795-00001`), not a numeric id.
- The system is append-only: there are no update (PUT/PATCH) endpoints. Corrections are made by marking leaf records `mistaken` and creating new ones. Lifecycle changes are explicit POST actions.
- A ticket's status is DERIVED from its issues and is never set directly.
- Workflow records (diagnosis, attendance, parts, pay, warranty, assignment, technicians) attach to ONE OR MANY issues via `ticket_issue_ids`, enabling flexible grouping.
- Every entity (tickets, issues, workflow records, catalog items, stores) accepts any number of polymorphic Notes and Attachments via dedicated POST endpoints.
- Notes are stored with their author (`created_by`), optional `type`, and may carry their own file attachments.
- Ticket closing notes are typed with FinalNoteType (`final_notes` or `what_we_learned`) and are appended via the `/final-note` endpoint.
- Issues (and tickets by derivation) now support a `cancelled` status in addition to the existing statuses.

tags.name: Issue Workflow
tags.description: Per-issue lifecycle: status, deferral, cancellation, assignment, diagnosis, parts, pay, attendance, warranty

tags.name: Notes & Attachments
tags.description: Generic polymorphic notes and file attachments — the same POST shape is available on every entity (tickets, issues, all workflow records, stores, catalog items)

tags.name: Daily Pay
tags.description: End-of-day payment records for technicians. One entry per submission covers any number of technician × store combinations (lines). Editing replaces the full entry while keeping a JSON snapshot of the prior state as revision history.

post.summary: Create catalog issue
Restore soft-deleted catalog issue
Add a note to a catalog issue
Add attachments to a catalog issue
Create technician
Restore soft-deleted technician
Add a note to a technician
Add attachments to a technician
Create category
Add a note to a category
Add attachments to a category
Create part
Restore soft-deleted part
Add a note to a part
Add attachments to a part

get.description: Lists tickets across every store. Supports the full filter set.

parameters.name: part_cost_single_gt
parameters.description: At least one issue whose summed (non-mistaken) part cost exceeds this

post.summary: Add a note to a store
Add attachments to a store
File a new ticket
post.description: Creates a ticket with its initial issue lines. Use multipart/form-data when attaching files (notes[].files or top-level files[]); application/json is accepted for field-only payloads. Notes supplied at creation are text-only — attach files to notes afterward via POST …/notes.

post.summary: Restore soft-deleted ticket
Cancel a ticket by cancelling all of its issues
post.description: Cancels every issue on the ticket that is not already cancelled, recording the provided reason on each status-change entry. Once all issues are cancelled the ticket's derived status becomes `cancelled`.

post.summary: Append a typed closing note to a ticket (multipart, optional files)
post.description: Adds one typed closing note to the ticket. A ticket can have as many as needed — post once per note. The note type must be one of the FinalNoteType values. Closing notes appear in the ticket's `notes[]` array filtered by `type`.

200.description: The refreshed ticket including all notes

post.summary: Add a generic note to a ticket
post.description: Generic (non-typed) note on the ticket. For typed closing notes use `/final-note` instead.

post.summary: Add attachments directly to a ticket

get.summary: List all issues on a ticket with full workflow history
get.description: Returns every issue with its complete history: diagnoses, attendance_entries, part_usages, pay_entries, warranties, assignments (incl. delays), technicians, status_changes, children (deferral chain), notes, and attachments.

get.summary: Show one ticket issue with full workflow history

post.summary: Set status on one or many issues
post.description: Bulk-sets the status of the listed issues; the ticket status re-derives automatically. 'deferred' and 'cancelled' are NOT allowed here — they each have their own endpoint that requires a reason.

ticket_issue_ids.description: IDs of the issues to update

status.description: Target status. 'deferred' and 'cancelled' are excluded — use /defer and /cancel respectively.

post.summary: Defer an issue — records reason and spawns a pending child
post.description: Marks the issue `deferred` and creates a new pending child issue carrying the same issue_id / priority / description, with `parent_id` set. The reason is stored in `status_changes`.

200.description: The newly created pending child issue

post.summary: Cancel an issue — records reason, no child spawned
post.description: Marks the issue `cancelled` (a terminal/finished status) and records the reason in `status_changes`. Unlike deferral it spawns no child. If every issue on the ticket ends up cancelled, the ticket derives to `cancelled`.

post.summary: Add a note to a ticket issue
Add attachments to a ticket issue
Assign issues to technicians on a date
post.description: Attaches the technicians to the target issues and moves each issue to `assigned`.

post.summary: Delay an assignment (snapshots old date into history)
Replace technicians on an assignment (not a delay)
post.description: Replaces technicians on the assignment's issues WITHOUT changing date/time — no delay record is created.

post.summary: Add a note to an assignment
Add attachments to an assignment
Add a diagnosis (multipart, optional files)
Mark a diagnosis as mistaken
Add a note to a diagnosis
Add attachments to a diagnosis
Add an attendance entry (multipart, optional files)
post.description: Clock fields are unconstrained — any subset may be set. The technician must already be attached to at least one target issue.

post.summary: Mark an attendance entry as mistaken
Add a note to an attendance entry
Add attachments to an attendance entry
Add a part usage (multipart, optional files)
Mark a part usage as mistaken
Add a note to a part usage
Add attachments to a part usage
Add a pay/driving entry
post.description: All money fields are optional. Attendance pay (base_pay / performance_pay, per hour) is separate from driving pay (driving_base_pay / driving_performance_pay, per hour) and per-mile rate.

post.summary: Mark a pay entry as mistaken
Add a note to a pay entry
Add attachments to a pay entry
Add a warranty (multipart, optional files)
post.description: expiry_date is required — it is the date the warranty coverage expires.

post.summary: Add a note to a warranty
Add attachments to a warranty
Attach technicians to issues (no schedule)
post.description: Attaches technicians to the specified issues without creating an assignment or changing their date.

get.summary: Download a multi-sheet Excel export of all data
get.description: Requires the secret-key auth header (not the bearer token). Returns a .xlsx workbook with sheets for: stores, tickets, ticket issues, status changes, assignments, delays, diagnoses, attendance entries, part usages, pay entries, warranties, catalog issues, technicians, categories, parts, attachments, notes.

post.summary: Mark a warranty as mistaken
post.description: Sets mistaken=true on the warranty. No body required.

post.summary: Mark an assignment as mistaken
post.description: Sets mistaken=true on the assignment. No body required.

post.summary: Mark an assignment delay as mistaken
post.description: Sets mistaken=true on the delay record. No body required.

get.description: Paginated list of daily pay entries with optional filters. Each item is a summary (no notes, attachments, or revision history — use the detail endpoint for those).

parameters.name: technician_ids[]
parameters.description: Filter to entries that include a line for at least one of these technician IDs (multi-select).

parameters.name: store_ids[]
parameters.description: Filter to entries that include a line for at least one of these store IDs (multi-select).

parameters.name: filled_by
parameters.description: Filter by the user ID who submitted the entry.

post.summary: Create a daily pay entry
post.description:
Creates a new end-of-day payment entry. Each `lines[]` element represents one technician working at one store. A technician who worked at multiple stores appears as multiple lines. Notes and file attachments can be included inline per line.

**Multipart form-data required** when uploading files. Field names use bracket notation: `lines[0][files][]`, `lines[0][notes][0][files][]`.

201.description: Created entry with all lines, notes, attachments, and linked ticket issues

get.description: Returns the full detail of a daily pay entry including all lines (with notes, attachments, and linked ticket issue IDs) and the complete revision history (snapshots of all previous states with who edited and when).

200.description: Full daily pay entry detail with revision history

post.summary: Edit a daily pay entry
post.description:
Replaces the full content of an existing daily pay entry. Before replacing, the current state is snapshot as a revision record (accessible in the `revisions` array). The same payload shape as create is accepted.

This is a **full replace** — all existing lines are dropped and recreated from the new payload. Any notes or attachments on old lines will not appear on the new lines unless re-submitted.

**Multipart form-data required** when uploading files.

200.description: Updated entry with new lines and a new revision record

bearerAuth.description: Sanctum personal access token issued by the auth service.

Trashed.name: trashed
Trashed.description: Include ('with') or limit to ('only') soft-deleted records

FilterStatus.name: status
FilterStatus.description: Filter tickets by derived ticket status

FilterIssueId.name: issue_id
FilterIssueId.description: Tickets that contain this catalog issue id

FilterIssueStatus.name: issue_status
FilterIssueStatus.description: Tickets that have at least one issue in this status

FilterPriority.name: priority
FilterPriority.description: Tickets that have at least one issue with this priority

date.description: The workday date for this pay entry (YYYY-MM-DD).

lines.description: One element per technician × store combination worked this day.

store_id.description: ID of the store the technician worked at.

ticket_issue_ids.description: Optional ticket issue IDs to link to this line. The technician must already be assigned to each issue.

notes.description: Notes to attach to this line at creation time.

files[].description: Files to attach to this note (multipart only — field name lines[N][notes][M][files][]).
Files to attach directly to this line (multipart only — field name lines[N][files][]).

DailyPayEntryResource.description: Full detail of a daily pay entry, including lines with notes/attachments/linked issues and the complete revision history.

revisions.description: History of all previous states, newest first. Each revision holds a full snapshot of the entry as it was before the edit.

DailyPayEntrySummary.description: Lightweight daily pay entry for list responses. Notes, attachments, and revision history are omitted — use the detail endpoint for those.

items.description: Summary line — no notes or attachments.

ticket_issues.description: Full linked ticket issue details with all relations.

DailyPayEntryRevisionResource.description: A snapshot of the entry's full state immediately before an edit was made.

snapshot.description: Full DailyPayEntryResource as it existed before this edit, stored as JSON.

edited_by.description: ID of the user who performed the edit.

Priority.description: Enum wrapper returned by the API for priority fields.

IssueStatus.description: Enum wrapper returned by the API for issue-level status fields.

TicketStatus.description: Enum wrapper for the derived ticket status. Never set directly.

FinalNoteType.description: Constrains the `type` field on ticket closing notes. 'final_notes' = general closing summary; 'what_we_learned' = lessons learned.

path.description: Relative path on the public storage disk

url.description: Full public URL — use this in / download links

files[].description: One or more files (max 10 MB each)

type.description: FinalNoteType value for ticket closing notes; null for generic notes on other entities.

type_label.description: Human-readable label for `type` when it matches a FinalNoteType; null otherwise.

attachments.description: Files attached to this note (e.g. a photo in a 'What we learned' note).

type.description: Optional free-form type string. On the ticket /final-note endpoint this must be a FinalNoteType value; on all other note endpoints it is unconstrained.

files[].description: Optional file(s) to attach to this note (max 10 MB each)
Optional file(s) to attach to this note (max 10 MB each)

from_status.description: Previous status value (null when the issue was first created).

reason.description: Required for deferrals and cancellations; null for plain status changes.

mistaken.description: True when this record was entered in error.
True when this delay record was entered in error.
True when this record was entered in error.

TicketIssue.description: A single issue line on a ticket, with its full workflow history when returned from the /issues endpoints.

issue_id.description: Catalog issue id; null when other_title was used instead.

display_title.description: The catalog issue title or other_title — always non-null.

parent_id.description: Set when this is a deferred child. Points to the parent TicketIssue.

Ticket.description: A maintenance ticket. Status is derived from its issues and never stored.

notes.description: All notes on the ticket. Closing notes have a non-null `type` (FinalNoteType). Generic notes have type=null.

issues.description: One or more issue lines. Each line uses either a catalog issue_id OR a free-text other_title.

issue_id.description: Catalog issue id. Provide this OR other_title, not both.

other_title.description: Free-text issue title. Required when issue_id is absent.

notes.description: Optional notes to attach to this issue at creation time (text only; use POST …/notes to add files to notes afterward).

issues.*.files[].description: Files to attach directly to this issue (multipart only — field name issues[N][files][]).

notes.description: Optional notes to attach to the ticket itself at creation time.

files[].description: Files to attach directly to the ticket (requires multipart/form-data).
